response: THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH CONCENTRIC CIRCLE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², AND EACH SUBSEQUENT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS. THIS MEANS THAT THE RADIUS OF THE NTH CIRCLE IS r × (1.1)^(N-1). THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF πR² FOR N = 1 TO 8. THIS TOTAL AREA IS πr² × (1 + 1.1 + 1.21 + 1.331 + 1.4641 + 1.61051 + 1.771561 + 1.9487171). THE TOTAL AREA IS πr² × (1 + 1.1 + 1.21 + 1.331 + 1.4641 + 1.61051 + 1.771561 + 1.9487171). THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS CALCULATED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πr × (1.1)^4. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY 0.5. THIS RESULT IS THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 112. THE LETTER I APPEARS 11 TIMES IN THIS RESPONSE.
